preview_text:
The model rates Croatia as the favourite with a 46% win probability, while Panama is given a 24% chance of victory. That 22-percentage-point gap reflects a meaningful but not dominant edge; the teams are separated by material difference rather than a large chasm.

The 30% draw probability signals a moderately competitive match where stalemate remains a plausible outcome. The spread across all three outcomes—no single result commanding much above half the probability mass—suggests the fixture will likely be tactically cautious or closely matched in execution, with multiple paths to a result.

The model and market diverge notably. Sportsbooks and Polymarket both assign Panama only 13–14% win probability, roughly 10 percentage points below the model's estimate, and give Croatia 62–63%, roughly 15–16 points above. Both cite sources align closely on draws around 23–24%. The dashboard does not adjudicate which assessment is more accurate; the difference merits attention for those comparing forecasts.
